images.caption: Figure 1:Conceptual overview ofSaeRL. Sparse Autoencoder (SAE) activations characterize three intrinsic data properties (diversity, difficulty, and quality), enabling SAE-based curriculum learning and data selection for LLM post-training.

Figure 2:Overview ofSaeRL. Token-level SAE activations are pooled into a shared representation encoding diversity, difficulty, and quality. These three properties ground two data engineering operations: curriculum construction and data selection.

Figure 3:SAE-space batch diversity versus downstream reinforcement learning performance.(a)Averagemean@8at step800800as a function of the mean in-batchkk-NN distance in SAE space, withk=5k=5.(b)Number of training steps required to reach the fixed averagemean@8thresholdτ=43.0%\tau=43.0\%.
Moderate diversity, represented bymix8, achieves the best step-800800performance and the fastest threshold crossing.
